$comment: Community-template (per-community D1 shard) migrations that THIS API commit requires.
This manifest travels with the API commit, so the web release can ask a precise
question before deploying: 'does every live community shard satisfy the schema
requirements of the API I am about to pin?'
It exists because nothing used to gate that, and it broke production twice:
1124 -> async post publish broken on 25 shards
1127 -> every publish failed: 'no such column: story_ip_metadata_uri'
and left one latent defect (1126, see below).
WHEN YOU ADD A MIGRATION THAT NEW API CODE READS OR WRITES, ADD IT HERE.
If the code path runs regardless of feature flags, it is 'unconditional'.
If it only runs behind a flag bundle, declare it under 'features' instead —
then the fleet only has to satisfy it when that feature is being enabled.

rewards.note: The outbox INSERT runs inside the study/karaoke attempt's OWN transaction (post-study-service.ts, karaoke-attempt-finalize-service.ts), armed by REWARDS_CAMPAIGNS_ENABLED && REWARDS_ACCRUAL_ENABLED. If 1126 is absent when both flags are on, the INSERT fails and rolls back the whole attempt — a fleet-wide outage of Study and Karaoke, which are LIVE and working. A clean read-only production verification on 2026-07-15 confirmed 1126 and the pinned unconditional requirements on all 94 allocated live shards; see docs/attestations/community-schema/production-rewards-2026-07-15.json. The rewards enablement workflow MUST still run the verifier with --features rewards immediately before either flag is changed so newly allocated or drifted shards fail closed.
